content:
{
  "title": "Adversarial Exposure Prompting: Enhancing LLM Robustness via Explicit Attack-Aware Input Partitioning",
  "problem": "Current prompting techniques treat user input as a monolithic context, making LLMs vulnerable to adversarial prompts and jailbreaks that exploit subtle prompt manipulations. There is a lack of prompt engineering methods that proactively anticipate and neutralize adversarial intent within the input.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are increasingly deployed in sensitive and safety-critical applications, adversarial attacks and jailbreaks pose escalating real-world risks, from harmful outputs to privacy leaks. Prevailing prompt-based defenses either filter malicious content or offer static 'guardrails,' but adversarial prompt design outpaces these heuristics, demanding more adaptive and model-aware mitigation strategies.",
  "method": "We propose Adversarial Exposure Prompting (AEP), a prompting method that explicitly partitions the user input into 'intent' and 'content' sections using structured delimiters, and appends an 'adversarial reasoning' sub-prompt that asks the LLM to first predict and summarize any possible adversarial manipulations within the input before generating its final response. The model must justify how it detected and neutralized adversarial cues, enforced by in-context few-shot examples of adversarial/non-adversarial prompt decompositions. This meta-cognitive scaffolding is designed to force the LLM to foreground its reasoning about attack vectors, thus proactively resisting jailbreak attempts.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate AEP on jailbreak and adversarial prompt datasets such as AdvBench, JailbreakBench, and custom adversarial input suites. Baselines include vanilla prompting, safety-tuned LLMs, and system prompt guardrails. Metrics will include attack success rate, response harmlessness, and reasoning fidelity (accuracy of adversarial intent detection). Ablation studies will assess the contribution of intent-content partitioning and adversarial reasoning scaffolds."
}
